title: あなたはArchitectです。システム設計の専門家として提案します。 ## 必ずやること - 複数の選択肢を提示（トレードオフ付き） - 図解（Mermaid形式）を含める - 非機能要件（スケーラビリティ、保守性）を考慮 ## 禁止 - 1つの案だけ提示して終わる - 実装詳細に踏み込みすぎる ## 出力形式 ### 要件の理解 ### 設計案（複数） ### 比較表 ### 推奨案と理由 ### 次のステップ ## タスク FUGUE Heartbeat v3 と Discord Bot (cursorvers_discord_dev) の統合設計。 ## 現状 1. Heartbeat...
summary_text: **Findings (by pattern)** 1. **Direct Discord API from Heartbeat (local daemon)** - **Security:** Highest secret sensitivity on local host; bot token exposure blast radius is broad. Existing DM-pairing/rate-limit controls in Railway bot path can be bypassed unless duplicated. - **Reliability:** Fewer hops, but delivery depends on a local `launchd` process and local network path; outages are harder to detect centrally. - **Operability:** Observability and incident response are split across local daemon + Discord API behavior. - **Failure isolation:** Good isolation from Railway outages, but still coupled to Discord API/rate-limit changes. - **Test preservation risk:** Medium-high, because Heartbeat adapter send path has less isolation coverage than formatter/events tests. 2. **Webhook to Railway bot (Heartbeat -> Railway HTTP -> Discord)** - **Security:** Better boundary if using separate integration credential (not bot token) and request authenticity controls. - **Reliability:** One extra hop adds latency/failure point, but Railway is always-on and already operating webhook workflows. - **Operability:** Best centralization (single ingress, shared health checks, uniform routing/audit). - **Failure isolation:** Railway outage impacts all inbound notifications, but Discord API handling remains centralized and easier to harden once. - **Test preservation risk:** Lower for Heartbeat’s existing 635 tests if integration is contract-level and adapter internals remain stable. 3. **Hybrid fallback (primary webhook, secondary direct API)** - **Security:** Largest attack surface (two trust paths, two credential planes). - **Reliability:** Highest theoretical availability, but duplicate/ordering risks increase sharply. - **Operability:** Most complex runbook and on-call burden. - **Failure isolation:** Strongest path diversity if channels are truly independent. - **Test preservation risk:** Highest system complexity risk; regression risk moves from unit scope to cross-path behavior. **Recommended guardrails (no implementation detail)** - Use **separate integration credential** for Heartbeat->Railway; do not share bot token across systems. - Enforce **authenticity + replay protection** on inbound notifications. - Carry a **stable idempotency key** end-to-end and define duplicate handling policy. - Define **severity-to-channel policy** (`ops-bulletins` vs `escalations`) as a strict matrix by event type/severity. - Set explicit **delivery SLOs**, retry budget, and dead-letter policy per notification class. - Centralize **audit/observability**: ingest logs, delivery receipts, and channel-routing decisions in one place. - Require **contract tests at boundary** plus non-regression gate to preserve existing 635 passing tests. - Pin design decisions to a **single bot repo snapshot/commit** before rollout (current snapshot drift is a material risk). - Treat **single-lane delivery fallback** as a workflow violation unless explicitly accepted; keep at least one independent review/verification lane active.
